articles.list_title: [DMR IMH2 MCP HSLE] M5A fmods - BIOS stays looping through the SMI handlers
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR IMH2 MCP HSLE] M5A fmods - BIOS stays looping through the SMI handlers
raw.description: Problem statement: BIOS remains hung and continuously fires the MMI handlers, for SMI: RasSmiHandler, PchSmmCoreDispatcher. Expected behavior: On a succesful case, BIOS sees 5 APM (0xb2) SMIs and continues execution, but in this failed state the handlers keep firing and 0xb2 writes are not observed in IDI. Observed behavior: BIOS remains in a loop and never boots Last messages in UART: PROGRESS CODE: V02010004 I0 Off-Board Video Active, Process PPI Request? Yes Scratchpad_Debug BreakAtCheckpoint: Verify Non Sticky Scratchpad7 0x52000000 Checkpoint Code: Socket 0, 0x52, 0x00, 0x0000 All AfterTrustConsoleEvent callbacks have returned successfully Unload A1F436EA-A127-4EF8-957C-8048606FF670 MmEntryPoint ... PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. PlatformHookPreSpiAccess () : SPI is currently locked down by BIOS. ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. MmEntryPoint Done MmEntryPoint ... ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. MmEntryPoint Done MmEntryPoint ... ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. MmEntryPoint Done MmEntryPoint ... ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. MmEntryPoint Done MmEntryPoint ... ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. MmEntryPoint Done MmEntryPoint ... ClearRasRegister: Success to get RAS policy. <continuously looping> Run-dir: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/orramire/DMR/MCP/ICI/v8/rev2_fmod2/fmods_SEQ_1_4T_C4M2.12
raw.subject: sighting
raw.comments: 14614720003 orramire We need to: 1. Disable SMI TCO_EN on both RTL and Simics. 2. Find a way to clean SMI_STS from Simics by reading the Simics value. This is not supported by shadower, shadower will read RTL value and prioritize it, but in this case it will leave a pending SMI in Simics.  14615054487 hmirand No update from submitter. Rejecting this hsd

raw.title: [DMR][MCP][HSLE][IMH2] SIGABRT after cold reset due to mishandled access to address 0x0
raw.description: Problem statement: Runs crashes after cold reset and during 2nd boot with a SIGABRT when BIOS issues a read to address 0x0, due to the crash all trackers are killed abruptly and logs can't be post-processed either. Observed behavior testbench.log: ^[[1m^[[34mrunning> ^[[0m^[[9D^[[K[oakstream_rio.serconsole.con info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xfff00bf3 240395155200474} [06:33:33.7702] Index: 13\r\n^M                    ^[[1m^[[34mrunning> ^[[0m*** object 'oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0]' is not executing^M Simics will now self-signal an abort.^M Last address in IDI hybrid tracker (no response with U2C_data or U2C_Response GO): |          8196515610 |           736702825 |      U2C_Data |                   |                   |      9 |        |      0x00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 |      |          8196515622 |           736702827 |      U2C_Data |                   |                   |      9 |        |      0x00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 00000000 | |          8199391503 |           737188132 |   C2U_Request |               PRd |               0x0 |     10 |        |                                                                                |      ~                                                                                                                                                                                                             ~                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      ~                                                                 Run-dirs: /nfs/site/disks/pse_oks_002/rrnavacu/HSLE/IMH2/M5A_EPR/VTC/SIGBART/mcp_ici_hsle_uefi_vtc_fmods_reset.1/ Exected behavior Run proceeds handling gracefully or causing a timeout for any address observed through IDI. articles.list_title: [DMR][HIOP][HIOP][MCP HSLE] Different Interrupt delivery methods are causing simics core double fault handlers
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][HIOP][HIOP][MCP HSLE] Different Interrupt delivery methods are causing simics core double fault handlers
raw.description: Error occurs when we run interrupt delivery methods 0-7. The script was randomizing the methods but i'm realizing now that theres a gap and there isn't a delivery mode for 3 which potentially could be causing problems. • 000b (Fixed Mode) – Deliver the interrupt to all the agents indicated by the Destination ID field. The Trigger Mode for fixed delivery mode can be edge or level. • 001b (Lowest Priority) – Deliver the interrupt to one (and only one) of the agents indicated by the Destination ID field (the algorithm to pick the target agent is component specific and could include priority based algorithm). The Trigger Mode can be edge or level. • 010b (System Management Interrupt or SMI): SMI is an edge triggered interrupt regardless of the setting of the Trigger Mode (TM) field. For systems that rely on SMI semantics, the vector field is ignored, but must be programmed to all zeros for future compatibility. (Support for this delivery mode is implementation specific. Platforms supporting interrupt remapping are expected to generate SMI through dedicated pin or platform-specific special messages).1 • 100b (NMI) – Deliver the signal to all the agents listed in the destination field. The vector information is ignored for implementations not supporting NMI-source reporting. NMI is an edge triggered interrupt regardless of the Trigger Mode (TM) setting. (Platforms supporting interrupt remapping are recommended to generate NMI through dedicated pin or platformspecific special messages1). For more details on NMI-source reporting, refer to Flexible Return and Event Delivery Specification version 6.0 or higher. • 101b (INIT) – Deliver this signal to all the agents indicated by the Destination ID field. The vector information is ignored. INIT is an edge triggered interrupt regardless of the Trigger Mode (TM) setting. (Support for this delivery mode is implementation specific. Platforms supporting interrupt remapping are expected to generate INIT through dedicated pin or platform-specific special messages).1 • 111b (ExtINT) – Deliver the signal to the INTR signal of all agents indicated by the Destination ID field (as an interrupt that originated from an 8259A compatible interrupt controller). The vector is supplied by the INTA cycle issued by the activation of the ExtINT. ExtINT is an edge triggered interrupt regardless of the Trigger Mode (TM) setting. run dir - /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_007/users/etsui/IMH2_vtc_model/msi/mmio_msi_dram_multicast/combined/random/onlyints/metro_basic_pushpop.3 #error [oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffea13c4 65357843481267} [21:19:52.2335] Exception 13 while calling the double fault handler. raw.title: [Pre-sighting->Sighting][DMR IMH2 MCP HSLE] - CBB CLAs never complete crashlog flow
raw.description: Failure observed running IERR-triggered AGR crashlog flow with  DMR-RIO 6 SimicsVP release (2026ww01.3.00_16), CBB CLAs are never completing crashlog flow ( "In progress" [30]  remain high): [00:20:05.176] E2E_LOG:: 13 wait for Booting to SVOS [05:55:19.043] E2E_LOG::  information after Booting to SVOS [05:55:19.124] E2E_LOG:: Watcher: 0x04000B01 [05:55:19.125] E2E_LOG::  - iMH0 [05:55:19.165] E2E_LOG:: 0x212917322A830123 0x000000C800000060 <= iMH PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:19.167] E2E_LOG:: 0x1333172402830123 0x00000E7000000370 <= iMH Primecode Crash Log [05:55:19.375] E2E_LOG:: 0x151522092E830423 0x000050BA00003D20 <= iMH OOBMSM Crash Log [05:55:19.563] E2E_LOG:: 0x153288422E830023 0x0000148A00017FF8 <= PeTe Crash Log [05:55:19.786] E2E_LOG:: 0x191883052A830023 0x000004000001D210 <= iMH S3M Crash Log [05:55:19.787] E2E_LOG:: 0x19790761 6 A830023 0x00004BE40001E200 <= CBB A PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:19.789] E2E_LOG:: 0x19790761 6 A830023 0x00004BE400031180 <= CBB B PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:19.790] E2E_LOG::  - iMH1 [05:55:19.792] E2E_LOG:: 0x212917322A830123 0x000000C800000060 <= iMH PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:19.793] E2E_LOG:: 0x1333172402830123 0x00000E7000000370 <= iMH Primecode Crash Log [05:55:19.877] E2E_LOG:: 0x151522092E830423 0x000050BA00003D20 <= iMH OOBMSM Crash Log [05:55:20.051] E2E_LOG:: 0x153288422E830023 0x0000148A00017FF8 <= PeTe Crash Log [05:55:20.257] E2E_LOG:: 0x191883052A830023 0x000004000001D210 <= iMH S3M Crash Log [05:55:20.259] E2E_LOG:: 0x19790761 6 A830023 0x00004BE40001E200 <= CBB A PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:20.261] E2E_LOG:: 0x19790761 6 A830023 0x00004BE400031180 <= CBB B PUNIT Crash Log [05:55:20.261] E2E_LOG:: Reset reason registers: [05:55:20.264] E2E_LOG:: GBLRST_CAUSE0 = 0x00000100 [05:55:20.266] E2E_LOG:: GBLRST_CAUSE1 = 0x00000000 [05:55:20.268] E2E_LOG:: HPR_CAUSE0 = 0x00000002 [05:55:20.269] E2E_LOG:: 14 Check BIOS FOR CRASHLOG. raw.comments: 14614960837 rrnavacu 22021243627 (child-parent) - link(s) are added via link tab.  22611695958 eeparks 22020679437 (child-parent) - link(s) are added via link tab.  14615088420 rrnavacu Kuprik, Ilya V Last updated on: Monday, February 9, 20263:35:13 PM(4 days ago) id:  22611753403 The issue is reproduced and isolated. As with similar reports submitted before, the root cause is a difference between simulated time and HW time, leading to the CrashLog Watchdog OOB timer expiring before all agents finish. We need to disable this timer for correct crashlog collection in VP: I prepared a script with all necessary settings for e2e crashlog with BMC/AWR  Simulated time is running differently on VP than on the HW platform, so the default crashlog watchdog timeout is not enough for CBB to complete its collection (CBBs finish the latest). In other platform configurations, timings may also vary, so the correct approach for VP is to disable the OOB watchdog timer. I added this to the script as well (I am setting the required watcher bits directly to disable it - OOB FW and HW processes the setting correctly).  14615088424 rrnavacu Tested script from  @Kuprik, Ilya V , CBB CLAs are now completing the flow. Only OOBMSM and Pete (not supported in SimicsVP) CLAs are failing: [20:27:36.748] E2E - iMH0 [20:27:36.751] E2E: 0x2129173281030123 0x000000C800000060 <= iMH PUNIT Crash Log [20:27:36.753] E2E: 0x1333172481030123 0x00000E7000000370 <= iMH Primecode Crash Log [20:27:36.758] E2E: 0x1515220985030423 0x000051BA00003D20 <= iMH OOBMSM Crash Log [20:27:36.772] E2E: 0x1532884285030023 0x0000148A000183F8 <= PeTe Crash Log [20:27:36.782] E2E: 0x1918830581030023 0x000004000001D610 <= iMH S3M Crash Log [20:27:36.784] E2E: 0x1979076181030023 0x00004BE40001E600 <= CBB A PUNIT Crash Log [20:27:36.786] E2E: 0x1979076181030023 0x00004BE400031580 <= CBB B PUNIT Crash Log [20:27:36.787] E2E - iMH1 [20:27:36.789] E2E: 0x2129173281030123 0x000000C800000060 <= iMH PUNIT Crash Log [20:27:36.791] E2E: 0x1333172481030123 0x00000E7000000370 <= iMH Primecode Crash Log [20:27:36.797] E2E: 0x1515220985030423 0x000051BA00003D20 <= iMH OOBMSM Crash Log [20:27:36.803] E2E: 0x1532884285030023 0x0000148A000183F8 <= PeTe Crash Log [20:27:36.815] E2E: 0x1918830581030023 0x000004000001D610 <= iMH S3M Crash Log [20:27:36.818] E2E: 0x1979076181030023 0x00004BE40001E600 <= CBB A PUNIT Crash Log [20:27:36.820] E2E: 0x1979076181030023 0x00004BE400031580 <= CBB B PUNIT Crash Log OOBMSM failure will be followed with OCODE FW dev team in 22021896933 - [TR][IMH2][DMR][CRASHLOG][MCP][Manual]OOBMSM fails on Manual Trigger

raw.title: [DMR][HIOP][MCP-HSLE] HAMVF logging hsf_miss_WbMtoIPush MCA when running supercollider VTC + cpu workloads to dram
raw.description: On MCP M5A model we are seeing HAMVF logging several MCA errors (hsf_miss_WbMtoIPush, uxi_illegal_wb), the first being WbMtoIPush. Based on the initial debug on the waveform at the time of the MCA, we are thinking that the WbMtoIPush is possibly from an eviction of a dirty line since we don't see any WbMtoI commands from HIOP with CF=1 near the time of the MCA. There are some strange behaviors at the beginning of the test that we can't explain, such as the CBB sending a WbMtoIPtl to imh8 hamvf without gaining prior ownership of the line. This doesn't seem to trigger any MCA and we recieve a CmpU back for the WB. This write also doesn't show up in the IDI trackers anywhere before showing up on the D2D tracker so i'm not sure where its coming from, but the address is in line with what we expect from the supercollider workload. Its possible that this puts the HAMVF in a funky state that causes problems for us later? #random WbMtoIPtl + CmpU from CBB0 without gaining owernship raw.comments: 14614991614 etsui Current debug signal list is here: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_007/users/etsui/VTC_MCP_IMH2_ww42/ddio/mca_debug.rc  22611728270 etsui Update from Arif in email Current finding : (1) HAMVF receives a WbMtoIPush request from an IOCA (2) it performs a lookup in the HSF to verify that the IOCA actually owns the cache line (CL) – HSF lookup miss or push miss is observed (3) tracing further down the line into HSF FSM – found that HSF.hitWays == 0 (Detail A) (4) Involving WAYS – may indicate that the CL selection at this point of failure got messed up – address decoding can be a big factor. Will require further trace/confirmation. Question : Is there a known issue or limitation within this model involving Address Decode ? Do we know ?     Detail A : HSF.hitways == 0 at failing point soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcmemright.memtopright.hamvf_0_0.parscfhaasf.hapar.hamvf_hacntls_wrapper.hamvf_hacntls.hatrkr.HSFFsm.McaWbPushMissU309H     Detail B : further trace required accounting for CLValid, CLState per partition and CLSelect.  22611729620 etsui Closer inspection of the ufi_d2d tracker and it looks like the rogue WbMToIPush transactions are likely the correct ones but the address/data are not being captured correctly. The WBs are where you would expect them to be for the WiLs from IDI and if you follow the previous InvItoE ownership request, you don't actually see the WBs for the correct address. Tracker HSD filed here - https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/article/22022037822  14615015408 etsui Update from Arif HSF Logic (Detail A) : 1. Tag matches (HSFTagHit=1) 2. Read CLStatePerPtn = 0xe3 3. Use CLSelect=3 to select bit 3 → value = 0 4. CLValidHitWays = 0 (no valid ways) 5. Decision: if (selected_bit == 0) → treat as invalid/no owner 6. Result: HSF miss, broadcast snoop   But: some cache has Modified data and is doing WbMtoIPush in response to a snoop request. Thus another cache (with different bit position) DOES have it !   Question_1 - Requestor-Owner Mapping mismatch : Do we know which bit position corresponds to the cache that’s initiating the WBMtoIPush ? If that bit is 1 in 0xe3 but CLSelect is checking a different bit (3), that is an issue. Do we know who can help here ?   Question_2 – CLSelect calculation mismatch : This is where we need confirmation on address mapping towards CL in this specific case. Can we do a hack to choose correct bit based on actual owner instead of requestor ?     Detail A :  14615015416 etsui Working on getting pythonsv register dump to check the programming settings for HSF address decoding. Looks like we need help from HSF design to look over waveform  22611763434 hmirand AI Generated summary from Chat discussion Summary of the Discussion Overall Context The thread centers on debugging a UXI cache-coherence–related MCA, specifically involving WbMtoIPush, EvctCln, snoop interactions, and unexpected HSF state updates. The investigation spans tracker logs, waveforms, and RTL signal analysis, with coordination between Tsui, Erikson, Gemini, Maya S, and others. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Key Technical Findings Unexpected EvctCln after snoop completion A core concern is how an EvctCln from IACA RNID 0x1 can occur after a snoop (RspI) should have already invalidated the cache line. The working theory is that the EvctCln was already outstanding when the snoop arrived, and it still needs to complete even after sending a snoop response. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] HSF state corruption linked to X-propagation Deeper RTL analysis shows that certain signals resolve to X, notably: RawHitPerPtnUp00H[0:0] = X This propagates to HsfIoRspHitWaysUp01H[5:0] = Xx These X values lead to incorrect IOCA way updates, causing EvctCln to clear more than just the requestor’s CL bits, which is inconsistent with expectations. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Pushmiss / HSF miss MCA root The MCA is triggered when a WbMtoIPush does not find any HitWays in HASIORsp, resulting in a pushmiss / HSF miss condition. Prior transactions (e.g., PushHint, RdInvOwnPush) are being traced to understand how HSF ended up in this state. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Protocol / Spec Interpretation Outstanding vs. blocking semantics The UXI spec (Section 5.2.6.1.2, “Blocking Snoop processing”) was referenced to understand whether an outstanding EvctCln should block snoops or vice versa. The conclusion from discussion is that, from a UXI perspective, this scenario may not be considered “outstanding” in the way originally assumed, depending on when the request is considered architecturally complete. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Sequence ordering clarification Based on tracker analysis, EvctCln is sent after the snoop response (RspI) is sent, reinforcing that this is not a simple ordering violation but a case of overlapping transactions. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Debug Status & Next Steps (as discussed) Waveforms were shared via VNC, and relevant signals were loaded into Verdi for further inspection. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] There is limited internal visibility between IOCA and HA; debugging relies mainly on IDI, HIOP UFI, and D2D trackers. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Priority was emphasized due to an approaching internal 1p0 milestone, with requests to continue high-priority debug on the associated HSD. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams] Bottom Line The issue is not clearly an illegal UXI sequence, but rather appears to stem from X-propagation in RTL signals, leading to incorrect HSF updates during EvctCln handling when overlapping with snoops. This ultimately manifests as a WbMtoIPush-related MCA. Further RTL cleanup or X-avoidance is likely required to resolve the root cause. [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams], [Tsui, Erik...on in chat | Teams]  22611791246 mzavalaa Hiram does this need to be cloned to the model tenant to make sure the fix is deployed? We need to make sure this needs to be looked at for every stepping/release.  22611792618 hmirand We need to make a beat analysis for preventing this escapes in the future. One way to catch it is with Coherency test and check with these 2 options: 1. Run VTC + core traffic targetting the same dram space addresses. Confirm you dont get miscompares or mca 2. Run Core traffic and confirm with the trackers that the traffic is not taking the scenic route  From: Gudino Gallegos, Alejandro Sent: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 10:41 AM To: Tsui, Erikson ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita ; Patil, Aaditya V Cc: Kapadia, Pramod S ; Miranda Morales, Hiram ; Ta, Quang A ; Zavala Almanza, Marcos Subject: Re: DMR MCP update to idi xtor with correct imh2 hashing adjustment values   Thanks for the info Erikson, will take a look at this. I already updated the values on M7 which is the next model that we will release and I see that we are reaching the same point, so you can expect this on the next EPR   Regards, Alex From: Tsui, Erikson Sent: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 12:39 PM To: Gudino Gallegos, Alejandro ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita ; Patil, Aaditya V Cc: Kapadia, Pramod S ; Miranda Morales, Hiram ; Ta, Quang A ; Zavala Almanza, Marcos Subject: RE: DMR MCP update to idi xtor with correct imh2 hashing adjustment values   Hey Alex, These values are defined by the fuses Hash_Adjustment_##. If you look at the values that are currently programmed in the default input dir, they are using UCC1 CBB0 values instead of UCC2. I believe for NWP and COR we will also need to make sure the hash adjustment values are set correctly, but I’m not sure if it’ll be the same IDI xtor doing the calculations or if another hybrid/simics component will be responsible. That will likely depend on how which model type we are running.   Note: Pramod said that for LUT[4-7], they mirror LUT[0-3].   https://docs.intel.com/documents/ClientSilicon/DMR_CBB/global/CCF/DMR_CBB_CCF.html#cbo-santa-and-cluster-enumeration    For DIE model, the fuses are driven by Simics like below [sim info] Fuse Recipe CBB Base0: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_cit_008/agudinog/collaterals/SIMICS_171/artifactory/simics-repos/ts/dumps/dmr/fuses/recipe/UCC_B0_Y25W29E0/UCC_B0_Y25W29E0/cbb_base/Q97G/s0d0_recipe.csv punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_00,2'h1 punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_01,2'h2 punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_11,2'h3 For MCP model this is driven by the IDI xtor which SHOULD take the values from below fuses but is hardcoded in the IDI xtor fuse_image_cbb/fuseinfo_base.log Direct: punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_00                                          = 0x1    # (FuseDefaultValue=0x0, addr=0x0af5, bit_index=3, width=2)) Direct: punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_01                                          = 0x2    # (FuseDefaultValue=0x0, addr=0x0af5, bit_index=5, width=2)) Direct: punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_10                                          = 0x0    # (FuseDefaultValue=0x0, addr=0x0af5, bit_index=7, width=2)) Direct: punit_fuses/fw_fuses_HASH_ADJUSTMENT_11                                          = 0x3    # (FuseDefaultValue=0x0, addr=0x0af6, bit_index=1, width=2))  14615151412 agudinog Besides pulling the changes that Erikson shared, what else is expected form model team here? Or would that be enough for now?  22611793080 hmirand We propose to add a test in the BAT model release to make sure we don’t get escapes in future models/projects With either one of these scenarios 1. Run VTC + core traffic targeting the same dram space addresses. Confirm you dont get miscompares or mca 2. Run Core traffic and confirm with the trackers that the traffic is not taking the scenic route  14615151661 agudinog Who will be providing those tests? Also what are the requirements to execute this tests that you mention? That would help us check if they need to be done on the PPR or EPR leve.  22611795624 jsingh3 @Tsui, Erikson - Besides answeing Alex's questions, what is needed from Model side to close this sighting?  22611801384 etsui I'm reviewing the current BAT tests being run on the EPR models and will provide a another BAT test if needed. Other than that I don't believe there is anything else needed to close this sighting.  22611801445 hmirand Not sure why it was moved to pss forum. This is a model issue where xtor didnt have the correct values. this hsd needs to be dispositioned as model issue and get a beat analysis on why it was missed so it doesnt happen again. Emulation and people time was used which it could have been avoided  @Redkar, Priya  ,  @Balasubramanian, Seetharaman  22611802218 jsingh3 @Tsui, Erikson - Send the BAT to Alex, Seethar and myself to review for BEAT. Based on your repsonse I will close this

raw.title: [DMR IMH1 B0 MCP HSLE] - For ECC Enabled run results in MCA error - 0xEE00000200A00090
raw.description:
Problem Statement:  For ECC Enabled run MCA error - 0xEE00000200A00090 is observed  from testbench.log [oakstream.serconsole.con info] {oakstream.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffffffff81a05bab 22013935628498} [18:28:17.6215] [    1.790635] ACPI: Added _OSI(Processor Aggregator Device)
 <oakstream.serconsole.con>[    2.500587] ACPI: 12 ACPI AML tables successfully acquired and loaded
 [oakstream.serconsole.con info] {oakstream.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffffffff81a05bab 22015354743629} [18:28:22.8335] [    2.500587] ACPI: 12 ACPI AML tables successfully acquired and loaded
 [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x159ecf53 2901834389} [18:28:23.4059] 2901834389 02/16/2026 18:28:23 RTI: Avg Wall Clk [avg_wall_clk.simics] emu.engine.wait-for-cycle -relative 50000000 [emu_mca_mon] MCA fired: soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcmemleft.memtopleft.mc_1.mcchanmse.parmcdp1.mcdata_wrapper_mcc.mcdata.mcdp_utils.mcdata_crnode.emu_mca_mon_inst   cycle count = 2906729907 [emu_mca_mon] MCA fired: soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcmemleft.memtopleft.mc_1.mcchanmse.parmcdp1.mcdata_wrapper_mcc.mcdata.mcdp_utils.mcdata_crnode.emu_mca_mon_inst   == 0xAA00000200A00090 [oakstream.debug_port.bank.backport info] {oakstream.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module1.core[0][0] 0xffffffff81941a4e 22015449964674} [18:28:27.7098] 0x0084 [oakstream.debug_port.bank.backport info] {oakstream.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[1][0] 0xffffffff81941a4e 22015491847004} [18:28:27.9667] 0x00ee Expected Behavior:   There shouldn't be an MCA error. Also, this is happening during OS where simics is accessing memory and not RTL. Path to run directory: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/acgarcia/DMR/MCP/IMH1_B0/V2.1/SEQ_0_4T_C4M2_fmods.11_DdrEccEnabled_Memtest_PP Impact:  With MCA error, is causing the instability issues sometimes boot gets stuck at OS.  Can't release PPR with ECC enabled.
raw.subject: sighting
raw.comments: 14615101889 vnaras21 Further adding the discussion on the email on this topic.  From: Narasipur Krishnachar, Vasantha Kumar Sent: Thursday, February 19, 2026 10:55 AM To: Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Garcia, Alfred C ; Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita Cc: Tan, Chee Kwong Subject: RE: MCA seen on MCP ICI IMH1 B0 V2.1   Thanks Arif,   On this point [Arif] Some Simics models may not fully implement ECC logic—check documentation for any limitations I suspect something on UMM not handling things correctly when ECC is enabled and not simics. Without ECC things looks good.     When ECC is enabled I see below unnecessary writes from xtors most likely from UMM when ECC is enabled.   [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4018] UMM: channel:1, dimm:0, rank:0, bank_group:0, bank:0, row:7, col:0 => sys_address:0 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4018] UMM: Final calculated physical addr from ddr_to_sys_addr function is 0xe000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4020] UMM: PHY2SYS: Final calculated system address is 0x7000000 from physical address = 0xe000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4021] write from flat  offset e000, 7000000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4021] void generic_ddr_memory_ex::write(ddr_addr_ex_t*, ddr_data_ex_t*): write data: 54 54 54 54 fe fe fe fe fd fd fd fd fc fc fc fc fb fb fb fb fa fa fa fa f9 f9 f9 f9 f8 f8 f8 f8 f7 f7 f7 f7 f6 f6 f6 f6 f5 f5 f5 f5 f4 f4 f4 f4 f3 f3 f3 f3 f2 f2 f2 f2 f0 f0 f0 f0 12 53 12 53   ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4778] UMM: channel:1, dimm:0, rank:0, bank_group:0, bank:0, row:7, col:0 => sys_address:0 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4778] UMM: Final calculated physical addr from ddr_to_sys_addr function is 0xe000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4780] UMM: PHY2SYS: Final calculated system address is 0x7000000 from physical address = 0xe000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4780] write from flat  offset e000, 7000000 [ddr_mem_3 info]  [18:40:09.4780] void generic_ddr_memory_ex::write(ddr_addr_ex_t*, ddr_data_ex_t*): write data: 54 54 54 54 fe fe fe fe fd fd fd fd fc fc fc fc fb fb fb fb fa fa fa fa f9 f9 f9 f9 f8 f8 f8 f8 f7 f7 f7 f7 f6 f6 f6 f6 f5 f5 f5 f5 f4 f4 f4 f4 f3 f3 f3 f3 f2 f2 f2 f2 f0 f0 f0 f0 12 53 12 53 --   This is from the mem test run area - /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/acgarcia/DMR/MCP/IMH1_B0/V2.1/SEQ_0_4T_C4M2_fmods.11_DdrEccEnabled_Memtest_PP   Will speak to UMM folks and try to check on this further.   Regards Vasanth     From: Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin Sent: Wednesday, February 18, 2026 8:44 PM To: Narasipur Krishnachar, Vasantha Kumar ; Garcia, Alfred C ; Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita Cc: Tan, Chee Kwong Subject: RE: MCA seen on MCP ICI IMH1 B0 V2.1     Do you know what “uncorrectable error for demand or underfill reads” means ? This error indicates that the MC detected an uncorrectable(UC) error during a mem read operation (either a demand read or an underfill read). Demand Read: A standard memory read request, typically initiated by the CPU Underfill Read: A read operation where the requested data block is not fully available in the cache or memory, requiring the controller to fetch the missing portion from memory.   Is this something like cache not filled for which read access is initiated ? Yes it can be. When a cache miss occurs, the CPU or system issues a demand read to memory. If the MC, during this fetch, detects UC ECC errors - it will report this MCA. In your case, since Simics is handling memory accesses during OS execution (not RTL), the error is likely being flagged by the Simics model when it tries to do memory read that returns corrupted or invalid data. often due to how the memory is initialized, configured, or modeled in Simics.   So, With ECC enabled run and access to memory from simics during os execution is causing this MCA to get fired. You are seeing the same behaviour in null run ? If so, lets move the debug there. Are you intentionally injecting errors? If so, are they within ECC correction limits? Are memory regions initialized correctly? If only part of memory is initialized, reads from uninitialized regions may trigger ECC errors. Some Simics models may not fully implement ECC logic—check documentation for any limitations     Arif Amin SNV DCPS East | DCAI   From: Narasipur Krishnachar, Vasantha Kumar Sent: Thursday, February 19, 2026 9:38 AM To: Garcia, Alfred C ; Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita Cc: Tan, Chee Kwong Subject: RE: MCA seen on MCP ICI IMH1 B0 V2.1   Hi Amin,   Decoding the MCA 0xEE00000200A00090 further, I see something not right with the memory read and seems its parity related.   Below is the MCA decode info.   0x000000a0 : mscod (31:16) (rw/v/p) -- Specifies a model specific error code that uniquely identifies the machine check error condition that has been detected.    0x0001 - CMI Request Address Parity Error (APPP)    0x0002 - CMI Wr da... 0x00000090 : mcacod (15:00) (rw/v/p) -- Specifies the machine check architecture defined error code for the machine check error condition detected: bit[15:0] = 0000 0100 0000 0101: Parity error on structures bit[15:7] = 0000 0000 1: ...   mscod RW/V/P 16 16 0x0 Specifies a model specific error code that uniquely identifies the machine check error condition that has been detected.           0x0001 - CMI Request Address Parity Error (APPP)           0x00A0 - Uncorrectable Error for Demand or Underfill Reads     Also, this MCA gets fired during OS execution, and during OS execution the memory access takes simics path and not RTL, as the memory related mapping is still in secondary space.   So, With ECC enabled run and access to memory from simics during os execution is causing this MCA to get fired. Do you know what “uncorrectable error for demand or underfill reads” means ? Is this something like cache not filled for which read access is initiated ? Is it possible to decode this further.     Pl let us know.   Regards Vasanth        From: Garcia, Alfred C Sent: Wednesday, February 18, 2026 12:21 PM To: Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita ; Narasipur Krishnachar, Vasantha Kumar Cc: Tan, Chee Kwong Subject: RE: MCA seen on MCP ICI IMH1 B0 V2.1   Adding @Narasipur Krishnachar, Vasantha Kumar since he is also seeing this issue on IMH2 M6A model with DdrEccEnable=0x1.   Btw, I have a run, but it’s still trying to generate the waveform capture.   /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/acgarcia/DMR/MCP/IMH1_B0/V2.1/SEQ_0_4T_C4M2_fmods.6   Thanks & Regards,   Alfred Garcia Pre-Si Valid/Verif Engineer (iVE PHG CED) Work/Cell: 619-884-1874 E-mail: alfred.c.garcia@intel.com “Knowledge is Power. Knowledge Shared is Power Multiplied.” - Robert Noyce.   From: Garcia, Alfred C Sent: Sunday, February 15, 2026 4:19 PM To: Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Olaya-cabrera, Margarita Cc: Tan, Chee Kwong Subject: RE: MCA seen on MCP ICI IMH1 B0 V2.1   Hi Arif,   Sure, I would have to run without -tardis_debug_disable in order to capture waveforms (ZPRD).  Let me do another run and share.    Thanks & Regards,   Alfred Garcia Pre-Si Valid/Verif Engineer (iVE PHG CED) Work/Cell: 619-884-1874 E-mail: alfred.c.garcia@intel.com “Knowledge is Power. mentions the following:   MCA: 0xEE00000200A00090, Error Source: Memory Controller, Error Name: Memory Controller Errors in 1LM mode Memory Read Error Channel 0 Error Description: Uncorrectable Error for Demand or Underfill Reads   Thanks & Regards,   Alfred Garcia Pre-Si Valid/Verif Engineer (iVE PHG CED) Work/Cell: 619-884-1874 E-mail: alfred.c.garcia@intel.com “Knowledge is Power. Knowledge Shared is Power Multiplied.” - Robert Noyce.  14615122477 molayaca No TI risk due to this issue, it has been root_caused to UMM not supporting ECC. Vasanth will add more details. With UCC disable and ECC enabled the MCA is not present. The support will be discussed with PESG, the owners of UMM feature.  14615128697 vnaras21 This HSD can be marked as root caused and "pursuing fix". Below is the summary of debug done and outcomes.  Summary:   Decoding the MCA - 0xEE00000200A00090 it indicates that error is caused during memory read operations during OS execution. Below are the details of this mca decode.     This error indicates that the MC detected an uncorrectable (UC) error during a mem read operation (either a demand read or an underfill read). When a cache miss occurs, the CPU or system issues a demand read to memory. If the MC, during this fetch, detects UC ECC errors - it will report this MCA. Demand Read: A standard memory read request, typically initiated by the CPU Underfill Read: A read operation where the requested data block is not fully available in the cache or memory, requiring the controller to fetch the missing portion from memory.   This is happening during OS execution and observing accesses on DFI xtor, which was unexpected:   I observe that DFI trackers are getting populated with reads and writes. The reason for this access is due to the below (Thanks to Arif for his inputs on this) OS simics issued a write to an address That address looks to be a cache_miss, thus it goes to memory MC will check incoming data with ECC in mind it initiated a read, sees smthing off and try to correct it correction fails - DUE or UC This is due to UMM not supporting ECC enabled transactions.  As observed could be observed from the DFI trackers below,  the reads are returned with ECC as 0’s and write from MC has ECC bits set but when the same is read, ECC has 0’s in it.   dfi_rcd_rdimm_xactor_1.rcd_rdimm_ddr5_xactor.ddr_xactor_chb.trk   |3936775291|3936775290|       0|   00|READ      |   000000E000|  0 |  0 |   - |  - |   - |   - |     - | 01 01 01 01 00        | |3936775409|3936775408|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E008|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | 02 02 02 02 00        | |3936775409|3936775408|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E00C|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | 03 03 03 03 00        | |3936775409|3936775408|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E010|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | 04 04 04 04 00        |   |3936775627|3936775626|       0|   00|WRITEA    |   000000E000|  0 |  0 |   - | 0004|     - |                     - | |3936775761|3936775760|       -|   - |WRDATA    |   000000E000|  - |  - |   - |   - |     0 | 54 54 54 54 FF        | |3936775761|3936775760|       -|   - |WRDATA    |   000000E004|  - |WRDATA    |   000000E00C|  - |  - |   - |   - |     0 | FC FC FC FC FF        | |3936775761|3936775760|       -|   - |WRDATA    |   000000E010|  - |  - |   - |   - |     0 | FB FB FB FB FF        | |3936775761|3936775760|       -|   - |WRDATA    |   000000E014|  - |  - |   - |   - |     0 | FA FA FA FA FF        |   |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E000|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | 54 54 54 54 00        | |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E004|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | FE FE FE FE 00        | |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E008|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | FD FD FD FD 00        | |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E00C|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | FC FC FC FC 00        | |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E010|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | FB FB FB FB 00        | |3936778249|3936778248|       -|   - |RDDATA    |   000000E014|  - |  - |   - |   - |     - | FA FA FA FA 00        |     To Further confirm this is UMM which is the culprit, we had disabled the UMM in our HSLE run. This boots to OS and we did not see the MCA. Here is the run reference - /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/acgarcia/DMR/MCP/IMH1_B0/V2.1/svos_32d14_ddreccenabled_umm_disabled/SEQ_0_4T_C4M2_fmods.0 Since IMH B0 MCP did not had the PCIe enabled (Currently WIP to enable it) had to go back to IMH2 MCP model to check if Supercollider test works. Had a run and the supercollider test also went thru fine with UMM disabled. (IMH2 M6A model with ECC enabled had same MCA found).  Supercollder test had failed with data mismatch when ECC was enabled. Here is the run reference - /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/vnaras21/IMH2_M6A_Model/SVOS_BOOT/metro_basic_pushpop.1_ECC_en_UMM_Dis_SVOS_SC_P Mem test with ECC enabled/UMM disabled also looks good. Here is the run reference - /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dcps_001/users/acgarcia/DMR/MCP/IMH1_B0/V2.1/svos_newsvos_ddreccen_umm_dis_memtest_modified/SEQ_0_4T_C4M2_fmods.0   Next Step:    If it’s a POR requirement to enable ECC , then we need UMM to support ECC. Other option if we need to WA with existing UMM Have multiple boot option with ECC enabled SVOS boot , Without ECC enabled SVOS boot specifically for VTd tests.  22611790405 jsingh3 3/3 -  Nitin , Vasantha, Jas aligned to leverage a feature enahancement hsd already raised by Nitin on ECC & UMM enabled Flow and close this HSD. raw.title: [DMR][XOS] No transaction after restore done with supercollider test on MCP V14 model
raw.description: Problem Statement: With V14 XOS MCP m﻿odel (6.14 kernel), we dont see any txn happen after restore done. only in simics Expected : We can trace supercollider txn on idi.log after restore done   Path to Logfile: /nfs/site/disks/pse_dmr_snv_008/mahmadsa/mousmi_v14/mimic/SEQ_1_4T_C4M2_bmods.2   Impact: PSS Coherency IMH2 Test case   Model: MCP HSLE DMR_A0_MCP_PPR_HSLE_25ww27_5 (  https://wiki.ith.intel.com/spaces/PPA/pages/4211724312/DMR_A0_MCP_PPR_HSLE_25ww27_5+DMR-MCP+SoC+ICI… ) DMR_A0_MCP_PPR_HSLE_25ww23_1 (  https://wiki.ith.intel.com/spaces/PPA/pages/4190619138/XOS+DMR_A0_MCP_PPR_HSLE_25ww23_1+DMR-MCP+SoC… )
raw.subject: sighting
raw.comments: 22611461882 jsingh3 9/11 - Rajeswari: GUOP trackers are being assessed for further debug  in collab with Mousmi (PDS XOS Co-owner)  22611467093 jsingh3 9/16 - Rajeswari: Medium sightings require twice a week updates, kindly update on the findings  14614627461 rviswana GUOP tracker is not showing the 5e8 memory access in supercollider run. In BAT test, guop tracker generation is not finishing as the file is exceeding the netbatch limit. As log2mem is working with supercollider, Safuan to run with that enabled to get supercollider logs to see the test progress.  22611477376 jsingh3  14614650007 rviswana Guop tracker processing issue is resolved by increasing memory size in the postprocessing script. Also, we are running few experiments : 1. supercollider command with log2mem to get the log. 2. Running to memory address in simics and reading it after RTL transition to check if the data is correct.  1667016021 eeparks There were recent changes to hello_world reference. #idi_hybrid_comp_s0_cbb0_Core0.transaction_shadower_io.input_space.add-map base = 0x3f8 device = idi_hybrid_comp_s0_cbb0_Core0.transaction_shadower_io.primary_space offset = 0x3f8 length = 0x8 priority = -125     oakstream.mb.bmc.espi.log-level 4     oakstream.mb.s3m0.log-level 4     $uart.input "xpressos-switch --switch;m wd 0x4e8000400 0xacedfaad;m rd 0x4e8000400;m wd 0x5e8000b40 0x8000050;echo 'hello world this is serial console working'"  14614667494 rviswana Basic XOS BAT test - with back door mem write and mem read after RTL transition showing good results : /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_cit_010/rviswana/safuan_exp/SEQ_1_4T_C4M2_bmods.0 Safuan ran supercollider(w log2mem) with 6.15 kernel so that we can check supercollider log, but the log doesnt seem to show full run. We are also seeing some differences in the run config between pure VP and XOS supercollider run. Safuan to check with PPR/kernel team. Mousmi running with wbinvd included to see if that could help with internal cache cleaning.  1566663234 mahmadsa With the latest run, we already can see txn on rtl Workaround : 1.adding -loop=10,-update_rate=1ms  and remove runtime parameter on supercollider command 2.adding stop run on script to clear cache 3.Using PPR official release image Wil reopen when issue occur again

raw.title: [DMR][XOS] Hit MCA fired reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412 after run supercollider with VTC
raw.description: Hit MCA fired reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412 after run supercollider with VTC. Test is run on DMR XOS model w/ VTC card enable. # testbench.log [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/15018665606] # MCA_DUMP.log soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 # Waveform capture [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/15018665607]
raw.subject: sighting
raw.comments: 1566666207 mmohdrif Hit MCA fired reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412 after run supercollider with VTC. Test is run on DMR XOS model w/ VTC card enable. # testbench.log # MCA_DUMP.log soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 # Waveform capture  1566670413 mmohdrif Update from RASIP domain From: Cortes Lepe, Alberto M Sent: Wednesday, November 19, 2025 1:01 AM To: Mohd Riffin, Muhammad Arif Amnan Cc: Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Ahmad Sabri, Mohammad Safuan ; Gutierrez Padilla, Jorge ; Escudero Osorio, Abimael Subject: RE: [DMR Coherency] Need help on MCA fired RASIP reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412 after run supercollider with VTC   Hello Arif Amnan, This is what we find: For MCA error of RASIP reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412: Bits Field Value (hex) Value (dec/bin) Meaning/Interpretation 63 VALID 1 Error is valid 62 OVER 1 Overflow: another error occurred while previous not cleared 61 UC 1 Uncorrected error 60 EN 1 Error enabled (signaling was enabled) 59 MISCV 0 MCi_MISC not valid 58 ADDRV 0 MCi_ADDR not valid 57 PCC 0 Processor context not corrupt 56 S 0 Not a recoverable error 55 AR 1 Action required 54:53 THRS_ERR_ST 0 No threshold tracking 52:38 CORR_ERR_CNT 0 Correctable error count 37 FW_UPD 0 Firmware did not update 36:32 OTHER_INFO 0 - 31:16 MSCOD 0x11 17 Model Specific Error Code 15:0 MCACOD 0x412 1042 Machine Check Architecture Error Code   Then going first to MCACOD 0x412: CMS and B2* erros (B2HOT, B2CXL, B2UPI) And from 2.4.2.69 reg_CR_MCACOD_list1 reg_MCACOD_list1_type: 0x1040 rasip_RASIP_ERROR_HANDLER_CR - reg_CR_MCACOD_list1@0x1040 (source) Description MCACOD value list holder register Consumer PCODE;UCODE BitWidth 64 SAI Policy Group .RASIP_P_U_CODE Field Name Field Access Field Width Field Offset Field Default Field Description MCACOD_B2UBOX RW/P 16 0 0x0 MCACOD for B2UBOX error MCACOD_B2HOT RW/P 16 16 0x0 MCACOD for B2HOT error MCACOD_B2CXL RW/P 16 32 0x0 MCACOD for B2CXL error MCACOD_CMS RW/P 16 48 0x0412 MCACOD for CMS error   So, this is a CMS problem: https://docs.intel.com/documents/arch_datacenter/DMR/Overview/DMR_Overview_HAS.1.0.html#io-switch-fabric Then considering MSCOD 0x11 (Contact: Monam Agarwal, Yen-Cheng Liu) https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/scf/HAS/Gen4/HAS/SCMS/R2204/HAS/SCF_GEN4_LATEST_SCMS_HAS.html#RAS The MCACOD value for SCMS/SCRS is set to 0x412. There are unique values set for each error in the SCMS/SCRS, identified by a 16-bit MSCOD value. The values are as defined below. The MSCOD values are carried in the MCA FULL BANK MSG based on the type of error encountered. Below are the unique codes for different buffer overflow errors: CMS MSCOD Values (source) Buffer Type MSCOD AD SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 0 AD TA 1 BL SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 2 BL TA 3 AK SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 4 AK TA 5 IV-GO SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 6 IV-GO TA 7 AKC SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 8 AKC TA 9 AD TGR or CSB 10 BL TGR or CSB 11 AK TGR or CSB 12 IV-GO TGR or CSB 13 AKC TGR or CSB 14 SASF_TX or SASF_RX 15   Let me know if you have any questions or further information.   Alberto  1566688472 ykhoo5 From waveform, found that the MCA is due to UBR Credit Overflow (soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.parscfubr_d2d_c1r3.ubr_d2d_cbb_tile.ubr_d2d_cbb.ubr_core.i_egress.i_nip_egress.ubr_credit_overflow_data_U107H[0].Valid).  Besides, found that credits to be returned (CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted twice) after being consumed (CounterCommitSpecuDecUnnnH asserted 1 time). CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted is due to AkcReturnCreditValuePerPortPerDestU202H. Next: Debug with DE Detail: A. Found that credits to be returned (CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted twice) after being consumed (CounterCommitSpecuDecUnnnH asserted 1 time). CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted is due to AkcReturnCreditValuePerPortPerDestU202H.  1566703684 ykhoo5 15018723898 (related-link) - link(s) are added via link tab.  1566703710 ykhoo5 Promoted to sighting and bugeco.  Bugeco is as below and it is still in debugged. Test is run on DMR XOS model w/ VTC card enable. # testbench.log # MCA_DUMP.log soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.paridfttop.root_rasip.rasip.i_rasip_registers.rasip_regs_inst.registers.reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0] ::0xEA00000000110412 # Waveform capture  1566692446 mmohdrif Update from RASIP domain From: Cortes Lepe, Alberto M Sent: Wednesday, November 19, 2025 1:01 AM To: Mohd Riffin, Muhammad Arif Amnan Cc: Khoo, Yong Sheng ; Mohd Isa, Muhamad Arif Amin ; Ahmad Sabri, Mohammad Safuan ; Gutierrez Padilla, Jorge ; Escudero Osorio, Abimael Subject: RE: [DMR Coherency] Need help on MCA fired RASIP reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412 after run supercollider with VTC   Hello Arif Amnan, This is what we find: For MCA error of RASIP reg_CR_MCI_STATUS[63:0]=0xEA00000000110412: Bits Field Value (hex) Value (dec/bin) Meaning/Interpretation 63 VALID 1 Error is valid 62 OVER 1 Overflow: another error occurred while previous not cleared 61 UC 1 Uncorrected error 60 EN 1 Error enabled (signaling was enabled) 59 MISCV 0 MCi_MISC not valid 58 ADDRV 0 MCi_ADDR not valid 57 PCC 0 Processor context not corrupt 56 S 0 Not a recoverable error 55 AR 1 Action required 54:53 THRS_ERR_ST 0 No threshold tracking 52:38 CORR_ERR_CNT 0 Correctable error count 37 FW_UPD 0 Firmware did not update 36:32 OTHER_INFO 0 - 31:16 MSCOD 0x11 17 Model Specific Error Code 15:0 MCACOD 0x412 1042 Machine Check Architecture Error Code   Then going first to MCACOD 0x412: CMS and B2* erros (B2HOT, B2CXL, B2UPI) And from 2.4.2.69 reg_CR_MCACOD_list1 reg_MCACOD_list1_type: 0x1040 rasip_RASIP_ERROR_HANDLER_CR - reg_CR_MCACOD_list1@0x1040 (source) Description MCACOD value list holder register Consumer PCODE;UCODE BitWidth 64 SAI Policy Group .RASIP_P_U_CODE Field Name Field Access Field Width Field Offset Field Default Field Description MCACOD_B2UBOX RW/P 16 0 0x0 MCACOD for B2UBOX error MCACOD_B2HOT RW/P 16 16 0x0 MCACOD for B2HOT error MCACOD_B2CXL RW/P 16 32 0x0 MCACOD for B2CXL error MCACOD_CMS RW/P 16 48 0x0412 MCACOD for CMS error   So, this is a CMS problem: https://docs.intel.com/documents/arch_datacenter/DMR/Overview/DMR_Overview_HAS.1.0.html#io-switch-fabric Then considering MSCOD 0x11 (Contact: Monam Agarwal, Yen-Cheng Liu) https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/scf/HAS/Gen4/HAS/SCMS/R2204/HAS/SCF_GEN4_LATEST_SCMS_HAS.html#RAS The MCACOD value for SCMS/SCRS is set to 0x412. There are unique values set for each error in the SCMS/SCRS, identified by a 16-bit MSCOD value. The values are as defined below. The MSCOD values are carried in the MCA FULL BANK MSG based on the type of error encountered. Below are the unique codes for different buffer overflow errors: CMS MSCOD Values (source) Buffer Type MSCOD AD SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 0 AD TA 1 BL SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 2 BL TA 3 AK SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 4 AK TA 5 IV-GO SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 6 IV-GO TA 7 AKC SCMS Egress or SCRS Egress 8 AKC TA 9 AD TGR or CSB 10 BL TGR or CSB 11 AK TGR or CSB 12 IV-GO TGR or CSB 13 AKC TGR or CSB 14 SASF_TX or SASF_RX 15   Let me know if you have any questions or further information.   Alberto  1566692448 mmohdrif From waveform, found that the MCA is due to UBR Credit Overflow (soc_tb.soc.imh9.subfcsouthcap.southcap0.parscfubr_d2d_c1r3.ubr_d2d_cbb_tile.ubr_d2d_cbb.ubr_core.i_egress.i_nip_egress.ubr_credit_overflow_data_U107H[0].Valid).  Besides, found that credits to be returned (CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted twice) after being consumed (CounterCommitSpecuDecUnnnH asserted 1 time). CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted is due to AkcReturnCreditValuePerPortPerDestU202H. Next: Debug with DE Detail: A. Found that credits to be returned (CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted twice) after being consumed (CounterCommitSpecuDecUnnnH asserted 1 time). CounterIncValueUnnnH[4:0] asserted is due to AkcReturnCreditValuePerPortPerDestU202H.  1566771759 ykhoo5 Promoted to Primecode Debug Support - https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/article/15018872697  1566834362 mmohdrif Currently completing a few ARs on debug session with primecode team. raw.description: Issue: (te mp) Some of crashdump related registers were not populated correctly. All these registers REQUIRED to complete crashdump flow These registers is updated during and by LTM construction flow. C onstruction flow is executed by firmware (uCode/oCode) when building or updating the LTM tables in OOBMSM SRAM These fields are used by the hardware to locate and access the correct core and ToR instances during crashdump collection. If these are not set, the crashdump flow will not be able to operate as intended. https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/oobmsm/HAS/Gen4/releases/R2203/LTM/LTM_Gen4_HAS.html#core-and-tor-crashlogcrashdump-control-registers sccg02040103> grep "AT: punit_telem_sram.crashdump_discovery.c" testbench.log   MAT: punit_telem_sram.crashdump_discovery.crashdump_supported 0x00000000  MAT: punit_telem_sram.crashdump_discovery.crashdump_supported 0x00000000 sccg0204010 if Crash Dump is supported. return 0 as Enabled [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.2041] emu_register_getvalue soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL  [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.2065] emu_register_getvalue done soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL = 0000000000000000 [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.4083] emu_register_getvalue soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL1  [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.4106] emu_register_getvalue done soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL1 = 0000000000000000 [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.4115] emu_register_getvalue soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL2  [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x2dcb44a 384148047} [11:49:54.4138] emu_register_getvalue done soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.paroobmsmram.msm_aggr.neb_registers.LTM_MSR_CONTROL2 = 0000000000000000 Observed that these registers were not updated Runpath: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_dppci_004/kasimmuh/CRASHDUMP/IMH2_AP2/m5c_ic_CRDMP_disablepcode.0 Question to asked? 1. Was ocode backdoor loading were properly loaded? 2. What Initial value set during patching?

raw.title: [Pre-sighting->Sighting][Reset][DMR IMH2 MCP HSLE] - ACM failure during boot and reset
raw.description: While running cold and warm reset flows with M5C model, we are observing "LT shutdown" event occurring due to ACM failure.  The ACM failure happens during both boot and reset, but the reset one caused a shutdown in M5C model.  No longer seeing the shutdown in M6A, but still seeing the ACM failure. [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/16029552411] Before it reaches LT shutdown state, there was an MCA and iERR was also followed which was happening around the time of icecode load. [image: https://hsdes.intel.com/rest/binary/16029552412] Cold reset: /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_008/eeparks/MCP_HSLE/M5c/reset_trs_rio/test_runs/22020413876_CR.0/ Warm reset:   /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_008/eeparks/MCP_HSLE/M5c/reset_trs_rio/test_runs/22020413864_WR.1   Pcode, Primecode, S3M trackers are available in the same directory. Please note that the LT Shutdown was not observed while running M5A, but the ACM failure was still there: M5A reference logs:  /nfs/site/disks/pse_oks_002/knshah/DMR/MCP_IMH2/RESET/PPR_version/logs/hsle/mcp_ici_hsle_svos_vtc_fmods_cold_reset.5

articles.list_title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP] First Cold Boot Fails to get through Cold Reset triggered by BIOS
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP] First Cold Boot Fails to get through Cold Reset triggered by BIOS
raw.description:
Booting with the following BIOS knobs changed:  DfxEmuBiosSkipS3MAccess=0x0;
DfxS3mSoftStrap=0x1, and GlobalResetLockEnable=0x00 Also enabled the RTC device. BIOS is doing the S3M Soft-straps handshake and triggering Cold Reset via CF9: Provisioning SoftStrap start... articles.list_title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][S5] S5 fails to complete shutdown flow
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][S5] S5 fails to complete shutdown flow
raw.description: Shutdown was completed in OS and SOC starts the S5 flow, but the HSLE modules never detect the conditions it is waiting for. hsle.simics is waiting for this to happen to detect that S3M got the sleep request: $io_space_pm1_cnt_list = (bp.log.wait-for object = oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space substr = "504 <- 003C0000") There are a couple of wait conditions that we use to detect the sleep request: $io_space_pm1_cnt_list = (bp.log.wait-for object = oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space substr = "504 <- 003C0000") OR oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].acpi.bank.ioreg.bp-wait-for-bank "PM1_CNT" -w value=0x3c00 I see the following in testbench.log: [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0x7d2c9b51 93976280304859} [21:17:29.6426] IO: 0x0000000000000504 <- 013C0000 [oakstream_rio.serconsole.con info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0x7f55d62d 93976274393230} [21:17:23.5181] PM1_STS: 0x0, PM1_EN: 0x120, PM1_CNT: 0x1C01\r\n The difference is the SCI_EN bit is set.  We should mask off this bit, because it does not matter for this trigger and could be set differently under different conditions. Here is the register definition for the PM1_CNT (Power Management 1 Control) register: Offset:   0x4 (IO Base Address: 0/31/2:BAR2) Size:   32 bits Default Value:   0x0000_0000 Bit Fields Bits Access Default Name/Description 31:14 RO 0 Reserved 13 WO 0 SLP_EN (Sleep Enable):   Write-only. Setting this bit sequences the system into the sleep state defined by SLP_TYP. Reads always return 0. 12:10 RW 0 SLP_TYP (Sleep Type):   Defines the sleep state to enter when SLP_EN is set. Typical mapping: 000 = S0 (ON) 001 = S1 101 = S3 110 = S4 111 = S5 9:3 RO 0 Reserved 2 WO 0 GBL_RLS:   Used by ACPI software to raise an event to BIOS. Write-only, always reads as 0. 1 RW 0 BM_RLD:   Scratchpad bit. 0 RW 0 SCI_EN (SCI Enable):   Selects SCI interrupt or SMI# for events. 1 = SCI interrupt, 0 = SMI#. Notes SLP_TYP bits are reset by RTC power-good reset. SLP_EN is write-only and triggers the sleep sequence. SCI_EN determines whether events generate SCI or SMI interrupts.

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][S5] S5 Entry flow hangs with errors
raw.description: S5 test failed with an IDI bus timeout, so I suspect that the IDI mux switching happened with an outstanding transaction (proximity to the mux switching message).  I won't have an opportunity to look deeper into the failure, but I've triggered tracker generation for debug. [emu.devices.idi_core_bfm_0 critical] {emu.xtorgroup_idi.engine 0x1e386723 8112206372} [02:40:41.2097] (advance_time) ERROR: Last transaction has timedout. Review in the tracker/logs where is the pending transaction in the flow.   /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_008/eeparks/MCP_HSLE/M5a/reset_trs_rev2/test_runs/22020679477_S5_enabling.2 Data from IDI tracker supports suspicion:  PortOut 0x504 are emulation cycle expensive, U 2C GO response for these take around 1.35M cycles.  IDI hybrid primary disablement happens at cycle 8102571531 which is only 0.96M cycles from the last C2U PortOut 0x504 request.  I'd recommend increase the delay for IDI hybrid primary disablement... Based on these inputs, made below changes (I am not sure this is the correct way to resolve) [image: blob:https://teams.microsoft.com/9eb505b2-288d-4728-9bfc-b660d9da84ad]   With this, it is no longer showing pending transaction error.   However, it is failing with IERR. logs: /nfs/site/disks/pse_oks_002/knshah/DMR/MCP_IMH2/RESET/S5/logs/22020679477_S5_enabling.0 [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x1e7c5d40 8183469043} [05:36:10.4331] CoreEventUpdate: setting the value for the caller emu.expr.trigger.trigger_9 (ExprTrigger)  [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x1e7c5d40 8183469043} [05:36:10.4350] [wait_for_expression_cmd][expression-triggered]id=252 expr=soc_tb.soc.imh8.subfcsouthcap.southcap1.parpunit.punit.ras.ierr_in_system_F002H == 0x1  [emu.engine info] {emu.engine 0x1e7c5d40 8183469043} [05:36:10.4940] [wait_for_expression_cmd][dec-cnt] id=252 cnt=0 [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x1e7c5d40 8183469043} [05:36:10.5650] [dmr_imh_forces.simics] Socket 0 imh 0 ::Result:: IERR Valid bit set! raw.comments: 22611559684 eeparks 22020679477 (child-parent) - link(s) are added via link tab.  22611564585 eeparks Re-ordered the S5 flow handling a bit, but still failing. /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_008/eeparks/MCP_HSLE/M5a/reset_trs_rev2/test_runs/22020679477_S5_enabling.1 7904593753 - Triggering S5 flow (start of test) 8097375744 - 1st SLP_EN bit set 8103609535 - CBB event for reset: PKGS5, Hybrid primary disabled, got S5 request message, SLP requested to S3M message (1st) 8103611166 - 2nd SLP_EN bit set (not expected) 8104109535 - power-button press to wake Simics (wait for Sleep indicator in S3M) 8104111535 - SLP request to S3M message (2nd) 8104965246 - Go response for 2nd SLP_EN 8107148454 - Cores disabled (MUX switched) 8113557500 - ERROR: Last transaction has timedout Getting 2 SLP_EN bit transactions, so need to find the source of the 2nd write.  2 suspects are the CBB ResetFSM or the SMI bit being set.  With try the ResetFSM part first and then try to clear the SMI bit.  22611567656 eeparks Suspect this is due to IO Trap mechanism that is not properly initialized.  Will open a new PreSighting with more precise information.

articles.list_title: [DMR][Reset][IMH2][HSLE][XOS][SimicsVP] SVOS reboot & shutdown commands hangs and does not initiate reset after XOS jumpstart
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][IMH2][HSLE][XOS][SimicsVP] SVOS reboot & shutdown commands hangs and does not initiate reset after XOS jumpstart
raw.description: Problem Statement: We are seeing a hang when executing reboot or shutdown after XOS jumpstart.  The failure also occurs in null mode (SimicsVP). Originally found on IMH1 MCP HSLE V8 XOS. Expected Behavior: Reset is triggered by OS through an IO Write to 0xCF9 (reboot) or IO Write to 0x504 (shutdown). Observed Behavior: Hang after reboot with IO Write to 0xCF9 not seen on IDI bus, so OS never triggers it.  Same for shutdown, where we don't see the IO Write to 0x504. Path to logfile: I don't have a path to a logfile, but it is easy to reproduce on both NULL mode and execution This is the command being used: $uart.input "echo acpi > /sys/kernel/reboot/type ; echo warm > /sys/kernel/reboot/mode ; xpressos-switch --switch;reboot" I was able to execute the reboot without XOS jumpstart in both NULL mode and HSLE: Impact: Unable to execute graceful reset flow using XOS.

articles.list_title: [Pre-sighting->Sighting][DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][S5] OS is sending 2 writes to PM1_CNT with SLP_EN bit set and S3M is responding to the first one
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [Pre-sighting->Sighting][DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][S5] OS is sending 2 writes to PM1_CNT with SLP_EN bit set and S3M is responding to the first one
raw.description:
Running S5 flow using shutdown command in OS.  This produces 2 writes to PM1_CNT with SLP_EN bit set.  S3M responds to the first write and sends GO_S1_TMP to the Punit.  Punit then distributes this and cores eventually get CPD_S1.  This CPD_S1 hits the cores in the middle of the IO Trap flow.  This is different from CF9 flow where S3M waits for 2nd CF9 before triggering the reset flow and sending the GO_S1_RW to the Punit. This is a big issue for the Hybrid model where we need to switch to RTL cores prior to CPD_S1, but after the core is done with the trigger event. 7912640542

The preceding comments are originally from the parent record 22021747443.  22611571185 eeparks Is this the correct behavior, or are we missing something in the setup for PM1_CNT register in either BIOS or OS?  22611582680 eeparks No difference between shutdown -P now and shutdown now, we have the same issue.  22611597103 eeparks Simics scripts were missing the first SLP request, due to the Simics message being different from expected.  The update scripts were able to trigger on the first SLP request and the RTL was able to complete the flow, including wake.  The Simics side was not working correctly, but this issue can be closed.

articles.list_title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][CR][SVOS] SVOS not performing Cold Reset when using reboot or shutdown commands
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][CR][SVOS] SVOS not performing Cold Reset when using reboot or shutdown commands
raw.description:
SVOS is performing warm reset instead of cold reset when using the reboot or shutdown commands. We are setting reboot_mode to cold and reboot_type to acpi, but still getting warm reset executed when using reboot or shutdown commands. The kernel’s command-line parameters — The Linux Kernel documentation reboot= [KNL]
 Format (x86 or x86_64):
 [w[arm] | c[old] | h[ard] | s[oft] | g[pio]] | d[efault] \
 [[,]s[mp]#### \
 [[,]b[ios] | a[cpi] | k[bd] | t[riple] | e[fi] | p[ci]] \
 [[,]f[orce]
 Where reboot_mode is one of warm (soft) or cold (hard) or gpio
 (prefix with 'panic_' to set mode for panic
 reboot only),
 reboot_type is one of bios, acpi, kbd, triple, efi, or pci,
 reboot_force is either force or not specified,
 reboot_cpu is s[mp]#### with #### being the processor
 to be used for rebooting.

 acpi
 Use the ACPI RESET_REG in the FADT. If ACPI is not
 configured or the ACPI reset does not work, the reboot
 path attempts the reset using the keyboard controller.

 bios
 Use the CPU reboot vector for warm reset

 cold
 Set the cold reboot flag

 default
 There are some built-in platform specific "quirks"
 - you may see: "reboot: <name> series board detected.
 Selecting <type> for reboots." In the case where you
 think the quirk is in error (e.g. you have newer BIOS,
 or newer board) using this option will ignore the
 built-in quirk table, and use the generic default
 reboot actions.

 efi
 Use efi reset_system runtime service. If EFI is not
 configured or the EFI reset does not work, the reboot
 path attempts the reset using the keyboard controller.

 force
 Don't stop other CPUs on reboot. This can make reboot
 more reliable in some cases.

 kbd
 Use the keyboard controller. cold reset (default)

 pci
 Use a write to the PCI config space register 0xcf9 to
 trigger reboot.

 triple
 Force a triple fault (init)

 warm
 Don't set the cold reboot flag

 Using warm reset will be much faster especially on big
 memory systems because the BIOS will not go through
 the memory check. Disadvantage is that not all
 hardware will be completely reinitialized on reboot so
 there may be boot problems on some systems.
raw.subject: sighting
raw.comments: 22611571200 eeparks 22021011570 (related-link) - link(s) are added via link tab.  22611571201 eeparks 15016550096 (related-link) - link(s) are added via link tab.  22611571213 eeparks We get a cold reset if we don't set the type to acpi, but this results in the write to CF9 of 0x02, which is known to cause an error (see 22021011570 and 15016550096): [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffffffff8125f619 100722615043073} [05:38:20.3312] IO: 0x0000000000000CF9 -> 00 [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffffffff8125f629 100722615220006} [05:38:20.3342] IO: 0x0000000000000CF9 <- 02 [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x20887f11 8733061373} [05:38:20.3414] [PPR] Reset type 2 [emu.engine note] post_cycle wait-for-time , emu.engine 5000000 cycles  [emu.engine note] Posted SIMICS event at cycles: 5000000, new_cycles 8739000141, alt_delta: 0, type: wait-for-time  [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0x7f86e870 100722622600188} [05:38:22.7815] IO: 0x0000000000000CF9 <- 02 [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x2089ccfb 8734429093} [05:38:22.7886] [PPR] Reset type 2 [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffffffff8125f63c 100722630097989} [05:38:25.2988] IO: 0x0000000000000CF9 <- 0E [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x208b28e0 8735854062} [05:38:25.3059] [PPR] Reset type 14 [emu.devices info] {emu.engine 0x208b28e0 8735854062} [05:38:25.3766] [PPR] cf9 iotrap count 1 [oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].pci_bus_mux.port.io_space info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0x7f86e870 100722637600188} [05:38:29.4718] IO: 0x0000000000000CF9 <- 0E Without ACPI mode, the CF9 is sent as 0x02 and the S3M will not respond to it: [idi_hybrid_comp_s0_cbb0_Core0.transaction_shadower_io.transaction_shadower info] {oakstream_rio.mb.s3m0.s3m[0].arc_uc 0x100c0bf2 20144527564578} [05:38:30.1212] transaction_shadower_instance::operation Completed WRITE mem_op to secondary memory interface, result=Sim_PE_No_Exception (1025) [idi_hybrid_comp_s0_cbb0_Core0.transaction_shadower_io.transaction_shadower info] {oakstream_rio.mb.mcp0.cbb_die0.tile0.module0.core[0][0] 0xffea0bda 100731573042513} [05:39:00.0329] transaction_shadower_instance::operation Completed WRITE mem_op to secondary memory interface, result=Sim_PE_No_Exception (1025)  22611574354 eeparks SVOS has these options set:  reboot=cold,pci,force  14614819399 eeparks The FADT contains a RESET_REG field, which points to an 8-bit register (typically I/O port CF9h on Intel platforms). To reset the system, software writes the value specified in RESET_VALUE (usually 0x06 for warm reset, 0x0E for global/cold reset) to this register. We will need to change the RESET_VALUE to do cold reset in the FADT part of the ACPI table.  I will need to find someone who knows how to do this.  22611597427 eeparks Test BIOS was created with change to ACPI table to do 0xe instead of 0x6 for reboot.  This test passed and produced desired results. BIOS recognized the cold reset without errors.

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][AWR] AWR hangs in exit flow Phase3 and cores not coming out of reset
raw.description: Executing AWR on IMH2 ICI MCP HSLE model. We are hung at this point in the dmr_imh_forces.simics script:    echo "Waiting for end of HWRS phase4 (FEATURE_IP_RESET_COMPLETE) for forcing the CONFIG_REQ/DONE for bringing ISA's out of reset"    @wait_for_expression("sle.simics", "{}{}{}{}".format(simenv.soc, simenv.imh_top, simenv.HIER_IMH_0_HWRS_TOP, "hwrs_treg_reg.registers.HWRS_STATUS.FEATURE_IP_RESET_COMPLETE == 0x1")) Cores seem to be stuck and not coming out of reset. /nfs/site/disks/ive_dmr_prednv_008/eeparks/MCP_HSLE/M5a/reset_trs_rio/test_runs/22021152462_awr.1

articles.list_title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][AGR] AGR hangs in early in exit flow waiting for SLP_Sx to de-assert
articles.subject: hsdes_sighting

raw.title: [DMR][Reset][AP2][MCP][AGR] AGR hangs in early in exit flow waiting for SLP_Sx to de-assert
raw.description: Executing AGR flow on IMH2 ICI MCP HSLE model.  Flow hangs early in exit flow waiting for SLP_Sx signals to de-assert by S3M. This is where we are stuck in dmr_imh_forces.simics:             @SIM_log_info(1, emu.engine, 0, "Waiting for SLP_S3/S4/S5 signals")             @wait_for_expression("dmr_imh_forces.simics", "{}{}".format(cli.simenv.HIER_SOC_TOP, "SLP_S5_N == 0x1")) Last few messages in S3M tracker log: 9106181981.0   | GLOBAL    | 0x809800f   | FW_STATUS  | [rt_lib]All RT setup done, going into event scheduler loop 9106381741.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xe6984ee   | FW_STATUS  | [bl_utils][IBL TREG] ibl_treg_set_ready_for_wakes: READY_FOR_WAKES = %d 9106445021.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xa898404   | FW_STATUS  | [acpi][ACPI] ACPI state = 0x%x 9106475341.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xa898413   | FW_STATUS  | [acpi][ACPI] is_glb_rst_with_vnn_cycle = 0x%x 9106517981.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xa898419   | FW_STATUS  | [acpi][ACPI] PWR_CYC_DUR timer started, reset duration: %d seconds 9170542221.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xe6984ee   | FW_STATUS  | [bl_utils][IBL TREG] ibl_treg_set_ready_for_wakes: READY_FOR_WAKES = %d Also seeing this message, which is unique from cold boot: 9097272541.0   | GLOBAL    | 0xa898134   | FW_STATUS  | [acpi][ACPI] INTL_ERROR detected This is the S3M source that outputs this message:             /* Check if the Global Reset cause was set by the FW, if not then it is HW related */             if (g_acpi_pmc_internal_ctx.last_gr_cause == LAST_GR_CAUSE_INTL_ERROR_OR_AGR) {                 TRACE_PRINTF_PUBLIC(BOOT_LOGIC_TRACE_ID_308, TRACE_SEVERITY_GLOBAL, "[ACPI] INTL_ERROR detected");                 g_acpi_pmc_ctx->mem.regs.gblrst_cause0.S3M_INTL_ERROR = FLG_SET;                 acpi_deep_gr_write();             } S3M eventually executes S5 entry after some processing of state (rtc_srv_go_to_Sx).  So, S3M is waiting for wake event.

raw.title: [DMR][HIOP][HIOP][MCP HSLE] Multistack hitting PCIETC_TIMEOUT on the Arden threads - due to VTC VM stopping execution of Vectors suddenly
raw.description: Tenant: sighting_central.sighting, Forum = pss.model .  Not in Scope = IMH SLE, MCP DFX SLE (Tenant = server.support). CBB SLE contact Pavel Mikhlin Description Field Section: Problem Statement: Expected: LCD VM0 continues to complete the rest of the programmed Vectors until it finishes and then sets the VM End Status to 1 Observed: LCD VM0 for some Ardens are suddenly stopping execution of Vectors and never getting to the point of setting the VM End Status to 1 Path to Logfile: Rundir: /nfs/site/disks/pse_dmr_snv_004/users/qata/imh2/mcp_multistack/manual/cross_imh/22020670195_hiop_p2p_cross_imh_base.3 Failure signature: ^[[?2004l^MERROR: stopPciLibAgent(/sv/arden-01/vm0): pcieTC_vm_checkDonePoll: PCIETC_TIMEOUT^M ERROR: stopPciLibAgent(/sv/arden-05/vm0): pcieTC_vm_checkDonePoll: PCIETC_TIMEOUT^M ERROR: stopPciLibAgent(/sv/arden-07/vm0): pcieTC_vm_checkDonePoll: PCIETC_TIMEOUT^M And final Upstream MEM Transaction is NOT the expected final vector: For arden-01 - dstack2 on imh8 setupIoAgents: Thread 0 will program thread 2 (/sv/arden-01/vm0)^M DEBUG: READ: vectorAddr: 0x164240000014, repeat: 62, addrAdv: 15, tag: 0^M DEBUG: READ: vectorAddr: 0x164240001014, repeat: 62, addrAdv: 15, tag: 0^M DEBUG: READ: vectorAddr: 0x164240002014, repeat: 62, addrAdv: 15, tag: 0^M DEBUG: READ: vectorAddr: 0x164240003014, repeat: 62, addrAdv: 15, tag: 0^M [INFO] [VM0] -------------------------------------^M [INFO] [VM0]  ARAM CONTENTS VM 0^M [INFO] [VM0] -------------------------------------^M [INFO] [VM0] Vector Type          -         Vec Addr    |  D-WORD 0   D-WORD 1   D-WORD 2   D-WORD 3^M [INFO] [VM0] UPPER ADDR           - 0x00001220c5000000  |  0x00000006 0x00000000 0x00000000 0x00001642^M [INFO] [VM0] MEM READ             - 0x00001220c5000010  |  0x3e000008 0x002000f0 0x40000014 0x000003c0^M [INFO] [VM0] MEM READ             - 0x00001220c5000020  |  0x3e000008 0x002000f0 0x40001014 0x000003c0^M [INFO] [VM0] MEM READ             - 0x00001220c5000030  |  0x3e000008 0x002000f0 0x40002014 0x000003c0^M [INFO] [VM0] MEM READ             - 0x00001220c5000040  |  0x3e000008 0x002000f0 0x40003014 0x000003c0^M [INFO] [VM0] INTERNAL ACCESS      - 0x00001220c5000050  |  0x56780063 0x12340ff1 0x0d4c0d4c 0x12345678^M [INFO] [VM0] ------------------------------------^M  - It is expected to finish its loops at address - 0x1642_40003F9  - The final MEM_RD that was sent out by the VM is:  7425526272 U      MEM_RD --- ---- 37E 5900 0035 ---- 0 ----  00001642 40000ED4  ---- - 0 0 0 --------------- 0 -     1 20000001 59000035 00001642 40000ED4 0 -------- -------- - 00 00 --- -- ---- ----  - Supercollider didn't try to stop the VM at all until significantly later (680,000 cycles later):  7426213298 D      MEM_WR --- ---- 32E 5800 0000 ---- 0 ---- 00001220 C50E5000 ---- - 0 0 0 --------------- F 0     1 60010001 58000000 00001220 C50E5000 0 -------- -------- - 01 00 --- -- ---- ---- Impact: 2 TR's from IMH2 PSS Execution Platform & Test Environment Section Failing Information: In case run command is not feasible to use Configuration is 8 LCD PCIe x16 cards connected across all MIO stacks Each LCD arden is programmed to target cross IMH P2P transactions (BAR4) with either 4 MEM_WRITE or 4 MEM_READ vectors that each have a repeat of 63 All of them are run at started at the same time in infinite mode, and after some time they are attempted to be stopped by writing the VM End Status bit Signature seen is that the MEM_READ vectors eventually just stop after 14 loops of walking the 4 vectors in infinite repeat mode. seconds -- Device Path: /sv/arden-03/vm0 B:D.F: c9:00.00 -- [INFO] Entering Polling Loop for checkDone [ERROR] Failed to poll for done (polled for)=5.009272 seconds -- Device Path: /sv/arden-04/vm0
